entry.title: Enhancing BW datasources based on ODP_CDS – useful for Datasphere too!
entry.content:
The need to enhance SAP Standard Datasources is a familiar requirement for a BW consultant. Traditional SAPI datasources are routinely enhanced using custom appends and exit/badi ABAP code or for Logistics datasources additional pool fields can be added via the LBWE LO cockpit.

While SAPI DataSources remain the mainstay of most BW systems, ODP_CDS Datasources have become more widely available. ODP_CDS is also widely used by SAP Datasphere and is at the heart of its SAP delivered Content.

I have recently found myself using ODP_CDS based extraction in both BW and Datasphere contexts. And I’ve found myself faced with that same familiar task – How do I enhance an SAP Standard DataSource? Same old question, slightly difference techniques.

Here’s how it can be achieved.

Data Extractor Extension using the Fiori App Custom Fields and Logic

The Custom fields and logic App is used for custom field enhancements (Z fields in old money). If a Z field has already been developed and belongs to the same Business Context as the extraction CDS view, the Custom Fields and Logic App can be used to make it available in your DataSource.

Let’s take DataSource 0C_BILLINGDOCITEMBASICDEX_1, which is based on CDS extraction view C_BILLINGDOCITEMBASICDEX_1 as an example.

Transaction SCFD_REGISTRY on the S4 system can be used to identify the Business Context that the Extraction CDS view belongs to (SD_BILLINGDOCITEM).

Use the Business Context in the Custom fields and logic App to find Custom Fields that can be included in your DataSource.

Select the desired custom field and navigate to the UI’s and Reports section. Use the “CDS View Description” from SCFD_REGISTRY (Data Extraction for Billing Item Basic) to find the corresponding entry. Enable Usage for this line. Save and Publish.

This generates 2 CDS Extension views. One for the main CDS extraction view (C_BillingDocItemBasicDEX_1) and one for the Association Extension CDS view (E_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEM) referenced on the Support of Custom Fields tab of transaction SCFD_REGISTRY.

The first view extends E_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEM which acts as a hook back into table VBRP giving a direct path for including additional fields.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZZ1_BFA9672D8D27'

extend view E_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEM with ZZ1_KOG7E7IFO4FCZHS5RBIOMCALBU

{

  Persistence.ZZ1_TESTEXTRACTAPPEND_BDI as ZZ1_TestExtractAppend_BDI

}

If you look at E_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEM it is a basic select on billing item table VBRP. Extension Associations equivalent to this are commonly used in many CDS views.

define view E_BillingDocumentItem as select from vbrp as Persistence {

  key Persistence.vbeln as BillingDocument,

  key Persistence.posnr as BillingDocumentItem

} 

The second view extends the main extraction view C_BillingDocItemBasicDEX_1, via the first extension view.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZZ1_783ADAFAD8A2'

extend view C_BILLINGDOCITEMBASICDEX_1 with ZZ1_4HYM4U74XYJM3IO2EQOIWB5VOM

{

  _ExtensionItem.ZZ1_TESTEXTRACTAPPEND_BDI as ZZ1_TestExtractAppend_BDI

}

A quick data preview of the Main CDS View shows that the new field is now included for extraction.

There are limits to this Key User Extensibility App approach.

Standard SAP fields cannot be added using the Custom Fields technique. If you are lucky, you might be able to use the “Data Source Extensions” option within the Custom Fields and Logic App.

But this will only work on Extensible CDS views which support this feature. Settings for this are configured under the Support of Data Source Extensions tab on transaction SCFD_REGISTRY.

The SD Billing Item CDS view does not support this, and it is absent from other Extraction enabled CDS views that I have checked.

If the Custom Fields and Logic App can’t be used, the CDS extensions will need to be manually developed in Eclipse. Which we will come back to in section Data Extractor Extension using Eclipse.

But let’s finish off the extension process for our Z field by updating the ODP_CDS DataSource definition on the BW system.

In eclipse, open DataSource 0C_BILLINGDOCITEMBASICDEX_1. On the Extraction tab choose Exchange.

Keep the existing Operational Data Provider and choose Next. New Fields available in the underlying CDS views and changes to existing fields will be presented. The new CDS extension field is listed at the end. Finish to implement the changes.

Check the DataSource to see that the CDS extension field is now included. Activate.

Now incorporate the new field into the BW data model by updating data targets, transformations etc…

Data Extractor Extension using Eclipse

If the fields you want to include in your Extraction cannot be added using the Fiori Apps, it will be necessary to add them manually using Eclipse.

A recent example I have worked on involved the extraction of Purchase Order Header data. The requirement was to simply add the field EKKO-FRGKE (Release Code) to CDS view C_PurchaseOrderDEX so that it could be extracted to a BW system and incorporated into an existing data model.

Taking a first look at C_PurchaseOrderDEX in the Eclipse editor you can see that it includes the extension association E_PurchasingDocument.

This allows us to follow the same extension technique that the Custom Fields and Logic App employed on the Billing Item example. Only this time we are creating the CDS extension views manually. As before, 2 extension views will be required.

First the Extension Association. To be on the safe side an appropriately named Alias should be used for the field that you want to include. A prefix of ZZ will give the extension some protection from possible problems that could occur during upgrades/support packs should SAP decide to include the field in a future release.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZZEMMPURDOC'

@EndUserText.label: 'Additional Fields for Data Extraction'

extend view E_PurchasingDocument with ZE_PurchasingDocument {

  Persistence.frgke as zz_frgke   

}

Then the main extraction CDS extension

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZCMMPURORDDEX'

@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for C_PurchaseOrderDEX'

extend view C_PurchaseOrderDEX with ZC_PurchaseOrderDEX

{

  _HeaderExtension.zz_frgke

}

There are currently no SAP provided ODP_CDS DataSources for Purchase Order data. So to complete the task create a custom ODP_CDS DataSource on CDS view  C_PurchaseOrderDEX.

Note on API State

It is worth reading up on API State Extend (Contract C0). Extensions are not supported for CDS views which do not have this setting. That’s not to say you can’t create extensions if this isn’t set. C_PurchaseOrderDEX which I have used here is not C0 released. Just be mindful that it would be wise to check extensions like this during upgrades. Using the ZZ alias name for any fields will protect your developments to an extent.

 

Useful blogs and reference material

As referenced in OSS Note 3005736.

https://blogs.sap.com/2018/01/11/extending-cds-queries-with-co-pa-ww-fields/

CDS view extension – long activation times

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003033180

entry.title: Building a Custom SAP Gateway Service with SEGW
entry.content:
Basics

In this blog series, we will cover the fundamental operations involved in building custom SAP Gateway services. These operations include:

  • Get Entity
  • Get Entity Set
  • Update Entity
  • Create Entity
  • Delete Entity

Deep Entity

Additionally, we'll explore more advanced concepts related to deep entities, such as:

  • Get Expanded Entity
  • Get Expanded Entity Set
  • Create Deep Entity

In this blog

  1. Creating the Custom STUDENT Table

  2. Setting up the SEGW Project

  3. Generating Runtime Artifacts

  4. Understanding Runtime Artifacts

    1. ZCL_****_DPC_EXT 
    2. ZCL_****_MPC_EXT 
    3.  ****_SRV 

  5. Conclusion

We'll walk through the process of creating a custom SAP Gateway service using SEGW. We'll start by defining a custom STUDENT table in SE11 and then proceed to create the necessary runtime artifacts to expose this data as an OData service.

Creating the Custom STUDENT Table

  • Navigate to SE11 and create a new table named STUDENT. Ensure that the primary key's data element type is set to 'CHAR' to avoid potential issues later on.

Setting up the SEGW Project

  1. Open SEGW and create a new project by clicking on the create icon.

  2. Enter the project name, description, and package details.

  3. Within the project directory, create an entity type by right-clicking on 'Entity Types' and selecting 'Create'.

  4. Specify the entity name and check the 'Create Related Entity Set' option.

  5. Double-click on the newly created entity type and enter the table name (ZRO_STUDENTS) in the 'ABAP Structure' column.

  6. Right-click on the entity and import the required properties.

  7. Select the fields needed and proceed.

  8. Designate a primary key field and complete the import process.

  9. Verify your project as it will have somewhat below like structure.

Generating Runtime Artifacts

  1. Click on 'Generate Runtime Artifact' and leave the model name as default.

  2. Following Runtime Artifact will be generated

Understanding Runtime Artifacts

We primarily work with the following artifacts:

ZCL_****_DPC_EXT

This class is where we implement CRUD operations, writing ABAP code to manage data flow.

ZCL_****_MPC_EXT

Here, entity type structures are created automatically. This is where we define our Deep Entity Type Structure.

****_SRV

This artifact is consumed in SAP Gateway Interface to create OData services.

Service Registration in SAP Gateway Hub System

  1. Go to Service Maintenance and double click on E8H_000 .

  2. Click on Register.

  3. Enter System Alias as per your configuration in your organization. For me its ‘LOCAL’.

  4. Enter your package and proceed.

  5. Click on ‘SAP Gateway Client’ in your service maintenance.

  6. Now we will be directed to new window with SAP Gateway Client. Here we will check if our project is working fine or has some issues.

    For now just click on Execute and check weather the response has ‘200’ Status code.

Note

  • When regenerating runtime artifacts, user implementations in ZCL_****_DPC & ZCL_****_MPC classes are reset. Hence, we utilize ZCL_****_DPC_EXT & ZCL_****_MPC_EXT to preserve our implementations.
  • In out next blog we will be move further and discuss the CRUD Operations.

entry.title: Re: The two project control possibilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Enterprise projects and professional
entry.content:
Hello, 

in our last InDepth session for this topic, we got many questions in our chat and we like to share the answers here.

Best regards, Sven

#

Question from chat

Answer from trainer

1

So, looking at the slide presented (around slide 15), Enterprise Projects cannot have Expenses assigned?

You can use WBS elements of enterprise projects to collect actual expense costs and through the shown XLS upload you can also plan them manually. But you can not manage them so far by help of the explained project demand object, that is only possible with prof. serv. projects.

2

Not a question but a "Congratulations" for the niciest presentation regarding this matter

Thank you very much 😊.

3

Does Cloud for projects do staffing for enterprise projects as well as professional service projects?

 

So far SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects, resource manage is only integrated with prof. serv. projects out of the box.

4

could I get the cloud for projects recording link

Link

5

can the resource planning be done on cloud or option not available?

 

Could you rephrase your questions and send it to me? sven.nachtigall@sap.com

6

on Enterprise projects would I be able to assign resources, do time recording similar to professional projects?

Yes you can assign resources to enterprise projects by help of resource demands (app “Manage project demands”). Once the corresponding project or WBS element is released, the staffed worker will see an entry in his / her timesheet task list.

7

Can Equipment be treated in a similar way as People in Customer Projects?

You can no assign individual equipment as you can assign individual worker. But you can plan in general the usage, e.g. by help of “Additional resources” in the “Plan customer project” app, on Work package level.

8

On Enterprise projects, Can you use a Cost Plus approach for billing? Like take costs posted and uplift them by % and bill the customer?  Like Resource Related Billing?

Resource related billing is not available for enterprise projects, only for prof. serv. projects.

9

Can you use Advanced Resource planning for Enterprise projects?  It seems that it is only direct assignment.

 

So far SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects, resource manage is only integrated with prof. serv. projects out of the box.

10

Gantt Charts for Projects : As per my understanding we have Gantt charts now available for EPPM projects but not for Professional services projects. When we can expect graphical representation of projects in Professional services.

Not planned at the moment. Feel free to raise a request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/930.

11

Change Request Management for Projects : Do we now have the capability to manage Change Request in Professional service project or EPPM project?

It depends on what aspect of a change request you like to manage. You could use different planning categories to show the impact on project financials or build certain workflows on your own. But there is no separate own business object for change requests.

12

Simple Projects  as a replacement for Internal Orders : Is there a special app to create simple projects in order to use them instead of Internal orders and how to create such a Simple project just to use as a cost collector.

It is the app I used in my demo “Project Control – Enterprise Projects”. The only difference to “complex” projects is that you do not create additional WBS elements by help of the app “Project Planning”, since the project header itself is a special form of a WBS element (which is different to OnPremise).

13

Budget Check : I understand that in case of professional projects there is no Budget check possible - like at WBS level in order for the project manager to manage the Budgets for each work package. If a customer wants to have budget check then we need to leverage only EPPM project . Is this still the case or there is any plan for introducing Budget check in Professional services projects.

Cost budget check is not planned for prof. serv. projects. For prof. serv. projects please have a look into the revenue “cap” available for billing element ( / sales order item). The attribute can be maintained for time & expense contract elements in “Billing” tab of the “Plan customer project” app.

14

Can I use App "Project cost report" also for PFS Projects to see planned cost vs. actual cost?

Yes can be used.

15

Can we send the invoice to customer for Approval? Is there any process defined for that

Please have a look into preliminary billing document, App “Manage Preliminary Billing Documents – Services”.

16

How to define Project billing in man-days/man-months

The E2E process for prof. serv. projects only supports hours.

17

any future scope for Budget functionality for Professional service project

Not planned at the moment. Feel free to raise a request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/930.

18

for ser based work package we use usage based contract type can we define seperate Revenue recognization for this type of contract?

The supported rev rec keys are mentioned here: link.

 

The rev rec key to be used can be configured here based on the contract type and product sold:

 

19

Do we have the option to manage the material in Customer Project?

You can for example create a purchase requisition or purchase order to procure a material and account assign it to a WBS element (= work package) of a prof. serv. project. But you can neither plan that by help of a project demand nor manage project stock with a prof. serv. project.

20

Do we need additional licences for Enterprise Projects? Or are these scope items also available with core product?

Enterprise projects (scope item 1NT) and prof. serv. projects (scope item J11) are both part of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud itself.

21

Please can you share where I can found out further information on where S/4HANA Public Cloud Projects functionality ends and S/4HANA Cloud for Projects starts.

In addition to the InDepth session from last week about SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud itself, please also have a look at this InDepth session to get an idea about SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects.

22

Since Enterprise Projects seem to do almost all which can be done via Customer Project, should not we consider Enterprise Project as default option for all customers? Apart from automation, is there anything wherein Customer Project does something which we cannot do in Enterprise Projects?

Prof. serv. projects come with a dedicated UI for this type of business. It also supports time & expense based billing, out-of-the box integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects and a planned cost & revenue calculation based on project demands. It is also integrated with solution orders.

23

seperate license for Cloud for project is required for staffing/ resource management?

Yes

24

Can a Customer Project, say on Time and Expense basis, be handled via Enterprise Projects also?

No, enterprise projects do not support time & expense based billing.

25

can we create PR/PO and assign WBS from Professional service projects?

Yes

26

Can we assign billing WBS element from Enterprise project to Professional service sales order item.

No

27

How we can define the detail WBS in customer Projects?

The work packages visible in the “Plan customer projects” app are WBS elements.

28

what is project billing new radio button for in customer project in prof service

By help of that, you can use the new project billing request and the app “Manage project billing”, which provides for example KPIs for your billing process, the possibility to write off or postpone items and a more flexible material determination.

29

Any future scope for creating automatic sales order for revenue enterprise projects.

Not planned

30

is this budget control issue resolve for project stock material while we create purchase requisition/purchase order

No this is not resolved. This is planned in the future.

31

can we use Account assignment category Q for project stock material

Yes, for enterprise projects with profile “Investment project” or “Project with revenue”.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP involved in refreshes that are executed via th...
entry.content:
Dear Hari_Bhaskar03,

When requesting the refresh from the tool, it will be automatic, but will always respect the threshold limit (Supported schema size of your source instance should not exceed the threshold limit of 400 GB of data. For Learning tenants, the schema size should not be more than 25 GB).

I hope this helps!

Thank you.

Best Regards,

Karen Perez

entry.title: Re: No batch input data for screen SAPLSPO1 0500
entry.content:
The text description of table OOKAQ 'CO Object: Control Data for Cost Elem. (Inttab for Enqueue)' is explicit.

The lock object sould be EKCOKAE (or S, look at SM12 which contains the nformation, lock object and key values) so you have to try to lock with ENQUEUE_EKCOKAE

  • When sucessful, unlock with DEQUEUE_EKCOKAE followed by CALL TRANSACTION
  • Try a LOOP calling ENQUEUE_EKCOKAE using wait option til successful, prevent infinite loop with a maximal number of try, then abort

NB: An OSS note advises against calling this transaction as batch-input - 314528 - CO-OM, CO-PC: batch input in Release 4.6 (but provides no BAPI, sad)

entry.title: Re: Extension fields in employee time sheet in Business ByDesign
entry.content:
@SandeepJanardan thank you for your reply, I have added it to customer influence - as below:

🔗 Customer Influence - ESS Extension Fields 

...but we all know this is a very slow route to any improvements - whereas customers need fixes/solutions here/now (and will likely workaround issues or migrate solutions before customer influence action).

I'm more surprised that 2 lots of loose text fields were chosen as the solution here - we all know that end-users are(n't) accurate in their loose text entries.

Would it take much to simply add 2x each of indicator, decimal and quantity - you then cover a wide range of extension scenarios, together with redundancy for more complex scenarios.

Loose text should rarely ever be used for any kind of analysis - whereas decimal, quantity, boolean (indicator) and lists (unachievable without full extension) can be compared and used for reporting purposes.

If you could consider this it would be greatly appreciated. I have little confidence in items from influence being picked up.

entry.title: 1S4 related error messages(4) :job failed Please create an incident on component FIN-FSCM-TRM-BF-2CL
entry.content:
You can use app 'Schedule Treasury Back Office Jobs' to schedule the background job to let the S/4HANA Cloud get the latest MRM market data automatically once:

  • The communication arrangement for SAP_COM_0203 is setup correctly
  • 'Assign... Notations' apps, you also do the setup correctly.  Below is the full list of the 'Assign... Notations' apps.
  1. Assign Currency Notations
  2. Assign Interest Rate Notations
  3. Assign Credit Spread Notations
  4. Assign General Volatility Notations
  5. Assign Basis Spread Notations
  6. Assign FX Swap Rate Notations
  7. Assign Security Price Notations
  8. Assign Exchange Rate Volatility Notations
    Please refer to below blog about how to correctly maintain Instrument & Property &Data Source in these apps:
    1S4 related error messages(3) : Request Current Market Data(TBD4): Request http status 204

In frontend app 'Request Current Market Data(TBD4)', you could get the market rate you want.

Steps to schedule a background job to :
1. Open 'Schedule Treasury Back Office Jobs'.

2. In step 1 , select the Job Template 'Request Current Market Data'. Then click on button 'Step 2'.

3. In Step 2,  specify when you want to run the job. 

You could also define the job recurrence pattern by click on button 'Define Recurrence Pattern'.

3. After the setting is done for Step 2, click on 'Step 3', specify the parameters for your job. And click on 'Check' button to check whether or not anything is not correct maintained.

Click on 'Schedule' and you will see the new job created.

And normally the background job should have status 'Finished' which means the job run is successful.  However, sometimes the job run status is not 'Finshed'. In below screenshot example, we can see job status 'Failed' and 'User Error'.  

You need to check those job runs whose status not are 'Finished' and identify and correct the error as soon as possible. Otherwise, you might not get the latest market rates such as FX exchange rates, reference interest rates, securities prices. And this will have a huge impact to your FI postings.

Some typical error types are : 

1. User Error: 'Logon of user CB998000XXXX in client 100 failed when starting a step'

Normally, this is due to the job run user failed to logon into S/4HANA Cloud, please help check whether or not the user is locked or the password is valid or not.

2. Failed: 'An error occurred. Please create an incident on component FIN-FSCM-TRM-BF-2CL'

There are many possible rootcauses for this type of error. But normally it's due to authorization issue. That is , the user has the authorization to run  'Schedule Treasury Back Office Jobs' and scheduled the job successfully. 

Then later on after many the successful runs of the job, one day, the authorization is mistakenly removed from the user. And the job run failed for all the days aferwords. In such case, please first check the job user's authorizations. The app 'Request Current Market Data' should be in a business role of the job user.

entry.title: Re: FAGL_FC_VAL SOLUTION (CUSTOMIZING)
entry.content:
Hi,

entry.title: Re: Suite Reporting Collaboration Hub
entry.content:
Dear Dominic, 

thank you for your question. Please refer to our Step-by-Step Tutorial on Process Model Dashboards in the SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub. It explains you in detail which dashboards can be found. 

In general you get the following overview:

  • Review key statistics on used, reviewed, and commented models
  • Usage of the current workspace

Hope that helps. Warm regards, 
Cäcilia

entry.title: Re: ABAP & http, why can’t we just have something nice?
entry.content:
Hi @ajith_urimajalu ,

yes we have a separate roadmap item for the 2025 on-Prem Release, see here: Integration Services | SAP Help Portal "Outbound HTTP/RFC: Connectivity Enablement".

The aim of ABAP Cloud is to also have a unified programming model for cloud and On-Prem. The existing programming model for outbound communication in the cloud will stay valid. Due to the different release cycles the feature will be available first in the public cloud, before it is available for On-Prem.

Best regards,
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Sync between SF Recruiting and otther application
entry.content:
Hi @-dk- 

you basically three options on how to integrate data with SuccessFactors Recruiting module.

  1. Use Legacy Recruting migration import from provisioning - this is applicable only for new instances for migration the data from another system
  2. Use sftp or rest connection from integration center - triggered by Successfactors, where it sends a request to your master ATS to obtain information from their API or from SFTP server in form of XML or JSON file
  3. Configure custom integration working with master ATS API and SuccessFactors OData API to synchronize the data between the two systems.

 

We usually use legacy import from csv on the go-live and than build a custom integration via OData API for our customers to sync information about job advertisements, candidates and their application, interview and other feedback or actions.

The OData API is well documented on https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/d599f15995d348a1b45ba5603e2aba9b/03e1fc3791684367a6a76a614a2916de.html?locale=en-US

Have a nice day, František

entry.title: Re: Archivelink question
entry.content:
Hi,

there is one solution for full automated integration of SAP attachments with any document management system, thus also with M-Files. That solution is a universal ArchiveLink connector and can answer all your requirements. It works with SAP ERP and S/4HANA on premise and private cloud.

Now I'm a bit surprised when you say  "Some documents will be created in SAP and sent to M-Files - these cases we know how to implement." because with ArchiveLink, this is really not obvious...

Indeed you need to:

1. On creation, capture the attachment binary and transfer it to M-Files + the necessary metadata retrieved from SAP

2. On Display in SAP, by default Archivlink is called... and then in the archiveling service you would need to call M-Files... 

Let me know if you wish to discuss this further !

entry.title: Inetum valora las certificaciones SAP para reforzar su compromiso con la excelencia y la innovación
entry.content:
SAP ha tenido la oportunidad de entrevistar a Ignacio Artiaga, director de Desarrollo de Negocio Estratégico SAP en Inetum. Inetum es una empresa europea líder en servicios y soluciones tecnológicas para impulsar la transformación digital. En la entrevista, Inetum esboza su camino hacia la adquisición de certificaciones SAP, discutiendo la importancia estratégica y los beneficios de las certificaciones. Inetum también describe cómo cubren todo el porfolio de soluciones SAP y tienen clientes en todos los segmentos de mercado.

Por favor comience describiendo su empresa y los servicios que ofrece.

INETUM es una empresa europea líder en servicios y soluciones tecnológicas, dedicada a impulsar la digitalización de empresas y organizaciones en los 27 países en los que opera. Con un enfoque centrado en el cliente, ofrecemos una amplia gama de servicios que abarcan desde la consultoría y el desarrollo de software hasta servicios de infraestructura y gestión de datos.

En la actualidad, la compañía cuenta con más de 28.000 empleados, una facturación global de 2.400 millones de euros y 2.700 consultores SAP, lo que nos convierte en uno de los grandes players en esta tecnología.

En este ámbito en concreto, cubrimos todo el porfolio de soluciones SAP y contamos con clientes en prácticamente todos los sectores y segmentos de mercado, siendo nuestras principales fortalezas el foco en la calidad, nuestra amplia experiencia en soluciones cloud y nuestro conocimiento de ciertos sectores y áreas funcionales, como Retail&CPG, Sector Público o Recursos Humanos.

Ignacio Artiaga, director de Desarrollo de Negocio Estratégico SAP en Inetum

 

¿Por qué decidieron inicialmente (en 2008) obtener una certificación de la categoría de socio SAP Outsourcing Operations Partner?

En 2008, decidimos obtener la certificación de la categoría de socio SAP Outsourcing Operations Partner con el objetivo de fortalecer nuestra posición como proveedores de servicios SAP de confianza. Esta certificación, a la que más tarde se unieron otras en outsourcing, ha consolidado nuestro compromiso con la excelencia en la prestación de servicios.

Contamos con certificaciones especializadas en Cloud and Infrastructure Operations, Hosting Operations, SAP BTP Operations, Business Suite Solutions Operations, SAP HANA Operations, SAP S/4HANA Solutions Operations y SuccessFactors Solutions Operations.

Asimismo, hemos adquirido competencias avanzadas en diversos campos, como Human Capital Management, con especializaciones en Compensation and Commission, Core HR and Payroll, Learning, Talent Management, Analytics and Planning, Application Development and Integration, Database and Data Management.

También hemos obtenido certificaciones en SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Customer Relationship Management (CRM) con especialización en Commerce, Customer Data Platform, Marketing, Sales, and Service, así como en Spend Management y Supply Chain Management.

Adicionalmente, nos enorgullece destacar que somos Partner Center of Expertise, lo que nos capacita para la venta de licencias SAP y su mantenimiento. Estas certificaciones y competencias adicionales refuerzan nuestra posición como líderes en el sector y demuestran nuestro compromiso continuo con la calidad y la innovación en la entrega de soluciones SAP.

Como hemos visto, desde la primera certificación han expandido el alcance de las certificaciones, es decir no solo han ido recertificando, sino que han añadido certificaciones adicionales. ¿Cuál ha sido la razón?

El objetivo de ampliar nuestras certificaciones SAP está enmarcado dentro de nuestra búsqueda constante de la excelencia y de la mejora continua. A medida que evolucionan el panorama tecnológico y las necesidades de nuestros clientes, hemos considerado esencial ampliar nuestras certificaciones para aportar un mayor valor a nuestros clientes.

La consecución de nuevas certificaciones no solo refleja nuestro compromiso con la actualización y la especialización en las últimas tecnologías y estándares SAP, sino que también nos permite acompañar a los clientes en una variedad más amplia de servicios y desafíos empresariales.

Al contar con un mayor abanico de certificaciones, estamos mejor preparados para ofrecer servicios más integrales y adaptados a las necesidades específicas de los diferentes sectores y líneas de negocio.

Recientemente han obtenido la certificación SAP BTP Operations. ¿Cuál fue el factor decisivo para optar a esta certificación? ¿Qué beneficios han obtenido con ello?

La decisión de obtener la certificación de SAP BTP Operations fue motivada por nuestro compromiso con la innovación en las tecnologías emergentes y nuestra aspiración de ofrecer servicios líderes en el sector. Un compromiso que viene desde 2017 con la creación del Centro de Excelencia Cloud, a través del cual, hemos centralizado actividades que incluyen análisis, prototipado, creación de soluciones personalizadas en SAP BTP y la implementación y soporte de proyectos en soluciones cloud, asegurando cobertura global y eficacia continua en este ámbito.

Por tanto, elegir esta certificación se fundamentó en el reconocimiento de que SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) representa un avance significativo en la manera en que las empresas manejan y aprovechan sus datos y procesos comerciales.

De modo que el factor decisivo para buscar esta certificación radica en nuestra determinación de ofrecer soluciones integrales que aprovechen al máximo las capacidades de esta plataforma. Con ella, estamos capacitados para proporcionar servicios especializados en operaciones dentro del entorno SAP BTP, lo que se traduce en una gestión más eficiente, mayor flexibilidad y un mejor aprovechamiento de las innovaciones tecnológicas para nuestros clientes.

Los beneficios asociados con esta certificación incluyen una mayor competencia para abordar proyectos complejos, acceso a recursos exclusivos e informaciones de primera mano, y la capacidad de proporcionar servicios más personalizados y adaptados a las necesidades específicas del mercado en el entorno SAP BTP.

¿En qué medida el mercado valora que cuentan con estas certificaciones?

El mercado valora positivamente que contemos con certificaciones en una amplia gama de áreas, desde operaciones de outsourcing hasta soluciones específicas como SAP BTP Operations. Estas certificaciones no solo respaldan nuestra capacidad para abordar proyectos complejos, sino que también indican nuestra disposición para adaptarnos a las últimas tendencias tecnológicas y responder a las necesidades cambiantes del mercado.

Por otro lado, las certificaciones SAP ofrecen beneficios integrales a nuestra empresa, fortaleciendo la credibilidad y confianza del cliente, resaltando nuestros puntos fuertes.

Además, ser un Partner Center of Expertise y contar con certificaciones en licenciamiento SAP refuerza nuestra posición como un proveedor confiable y autorizado. Esto no solo es crucial para la confianza de los clientes, sino que también puede influir en las decisiones de compra, ya que las organizaciones buscan socios que no solo ofrezcan soluciones tecnológicas avanzadas, sino que también estén respaldadas por las certificaciones y competencias necesarias.

¿Qué beneficios ven en las certificaciones de SAP?

Las certificaciones de SAP ofrecen una serie de beneficios significativos que impactan positivamente en diversas áreas de nuestras operaciones.

Por ejemplo, durante las auditorías, las certificaciones proporcionan una validación objetiva de nuestro cumplimiento con los estándares y prácticas recomendadas, asegurando la calidad y eficiencia de nuestros servicios. Las recomendaciones resultantes de estas auditorías nos brindan oportunidades para mejorar continuamente, afinar nuestras operaciones y mantenernos al día con las últimas mejores prácticas del sector.

Más allá de validar nuestro cumplimiento con estándares y prácticas recomendadas durante auditorías, estas certificaciones contribuyen al desarrollo y retención del talento, mejoran la eficiencia operativa y nos permiten alinearnos de manera más efectiva con las estrategias empresariales de nuestros clientes.

En última instancia, estas credenciales son un activo estratégico que respalda nuestro compromiso con la excelencia, la innovación y el éxito a largo plazo en un entorno digital dinámico.

entry.title: Managing Profit Centres & Profit Centre Groups
entry.content:
Agenda of this blog is as follows: 

  • Profit Centre Configuration
  • Create Profit Centre Groups
  • Create Profit Centres

Profit Centre Configuration

The standard Hierarchy is a special type of profit centres group that contains all profit centres belonging to a controlling area and that reflects the organizational structure in Profit Centres Accounting. This is where you check your configuration related to Profit Centres.

Prerequisites:

App used: Manage Your Solution

SAP Business Role Required: SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT

To validate the configuration of Profit Centre, please follow the below steps:

  1. Search for the Manage Your Solution App
  2. Click on Configure Your Solution
  3. Select the Finance application area and the Master and Organizational Data sub area as shown below
  4. Click on Configure Profit Centre Item
  5. Here you will see Standard Hierarchy ‘YBPH’ is assigned to pre-configured controlling area ‘A000’. 
Note: 
  • Standard Hierarchy ‘YBPH’ is assigned to pre-configured controlling area ‘A000’. If you do not want to use the SAP’s default value, make sure to maintain assignment of the new value in ‘SAP Central Business Configuration > Configuration Activities > Define Profit Centre Standard Hierarchy in Controlling Area’. Reference task Transport Configuration to move the setting to Test and Production System.
  • If you want to rename the Profit Centre Group you can edit the name accordingly.

 -------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create Profit Centre Groups

You can use this app to search for profit centre groups as well as create, display, and edit profit centre groups.

Prerequisites:

App used: Manage Profit Centre Groups

SAP Business Role Required: SAP_BR_DIVISION_ACCOUNTANT

In the Realize Phase, during the setup of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system, you must create a default profit centre and a default profit centre group in your quality system. Since master data changes are not transported to your production system, you must recreate these objects in your production system before you commence business. You create these master data objects in both systems using the Manage Profit Centres and Manage Profit Centre Groups, respectively.

Note: that you cannot create versions for the standard Hierarchy as a special profit centre group.

So now let us see how to create the Profit Centre Groups for the first time:

  1. Go to Manage Profit Centre Group App
  2. Filter with Controlling Area: A000 and Click on Go
  3. For the First time “In SAP’s pre-delivered standard content, a default profit centre standard hierarchy YBPH is already assigned to the controlling area A000. However, the profit centre group YBPH is not delivered since it belongs to the profit centre master data. So you need to create by Clicking on New button
  4. Enter the Name as of your above configuration(Profit Centre Configuration), I have chosen as A you can use YBPH itself and Click on Continue 
  5. Accordingly, you go ahead creating & adding the Child Groups

How to deal with the existing Profit Centre Group Scenario:

  1. Let’s say your standard Hierarchy Node is YBPH
  2. You have created new profit centre groups and those are not available for the selection during profit centre creation 
  3. Reason is Newly created Profit centre group is not created under Standard Hierarchy i.e. YBPH
  4. Let me reproduce above issue mentioned in the step 2 Here I am creating the Profit Centre Group i.e. TestAk which is not under YBPH
  5. Here I have created Profit Centre and child Profit centre group
  6. Now let me go to Manage Profit Centre App and create new profit centre with this Profit centre group
  7. So now how to fix this issue, let's continue..
  8. To make the new profit centre group created available for selection, you go ahead and create new child groups under standard delivered Hierarchy node i.e. YBPH
  9. Select Standard Hierarchy Node YBPH & Click on Edit
  10.  Click o New Child Group, lets add 2 child groups Atest1 & Atest2

 After this creation, I am expecting to see these 2 Profit centre Groups under value selection of Standard Hierarchy Node during Profit centre creation as shown below: 

Further you can refer the below video for your reference.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create Profit Centre

App used: Manage Profit Centre 

SAP Business Role Required: SAP_BR_DIVISION_ACCOUNTANT

Use this app to search for, create, view, and edit profit center master data. This app allows you to manage profit center master data in an easy-to-consume way.

In short in this app, you can Create, edit, copy, and delete a combination of a profit center and a validity period.

To create Profit Centre follow the below steps:

  1. Go to Manage Profit Centre app
  2. Enter the filters and look for any existing profit centres, if not click on Create button
  3. Fill the header information like Profit Center ID, Profit Centre name, Valid from, Valid  to, Person responsible, set the organizational unit details like Standard Hierarchy Node & Segment all these are mandatory fields and other informations. Here I have used the same Profit Centre Group i.e. Atest1 which is created as above section
  4. Click on Create

Important Links:

Help Portal:

Manage Profit Centers

Manage Profit Center Groups

Community:

Profit Centre is not creation in S/4 HANA Public Cloud

Knowledge Base Article(KBA):

Newly created Profit Centre Group not visible in app 'Manage Profit Centres'

Roadmap Viewer:

SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2-system landscape)

Regards,

Ankit K

entry.title: Re: Ask and Comment About the Community Platform and Migration
entry.content:
Hi Jerry, 

Thanks for getting back to me. I've only ever had one ID, so there's no merge issue. 

Initially some blogs I definitely knew were not to be found. Some have now 'surfaced'. It seems like once I find and open them via Google then SCN search finds them too. According to my profile stats I've published 15 blogs. Trying to access them I hit against a number of issues:

  1. I can't see my blogs under my content. All I can see is "Posts". No blogs or ability to filter by blogs.


  2. If I search for my user or surname and type "Blog" it finds 3. See previous point, I can't think of or find any other way to search.


  3. The pages render randomly either shrunken or scrolled to the left or right (Safari on macOS, latest). The screenshot above is not scaled down, it's how it renders. Sometimes you see the page in original size for a split second before it does its thing. 

  4. "If you look at the Blog Dashboard box on that page, you should see View My Contributions." --> There is neither a Blog Dashboard Box nor anything labelled My Contributions on that page. I even tried searching on "Dashboard" and "Contribution".

I deliberately left a couple of weeks of 'settling in' before taking a peek at the new platform. I had high hopes, as the last platform was the main reason I moved away from SCN. But sadly things are not off to a good start. I'll keep trying and post further issues in the other blog you mentioned. At least we can now paste images like we could do two platforms ago, so that's a huge step forward.

Thanks & regards,
Mike

entry.title: Re: Report header and extended attribute
entry.content:
Hi Alexandre,

you can use Entity Card to display ext.attribute values of Entities. See attached screenshot Entity.png with extended attribute "Attribute_1". You can also modify Model Card to display your customized stuff, but you cannot have any logic there, meaning you probably have multiple entities in your model and your report cannot resolve, which extended attribute of which entity you need to use. So in this case you need to create for example computed extended attribute at the model level and this extended attribute can be then displayed on the Model Card (green parts on the Model.png screenshot) or in separate Text paragraph (red parts on the Model.png screenshot).

However if you talk about modifying the Title page in the Report Properties, possibilities are very limited and I don`t know about any way, how to get extended attributes here. I`d suggest replacing the predefined Home page with your own page.

HTH,

Ondrej Divis

thanks for the feedback.

In fact, we are currently working to also allow general purpose SCIM connectors directly with bundled SAP Cloud Identity  services - Identity Provisioning tenants. This will be a priced feature, though. Only connectivity towards SAP systems is available at no additional cost.

For your second question you are right, there's no other SAP on-premise product for identity management.

Michael

entry.title: Re: Stored Procedure Notification
entry.content:
Hi,

You will have to write a TN on journal entry and it will trigger at every transaction. You can give a check on this. I do not have system in front of me to check before giving it to you. Apologies in case of any error.

IF :object_type = '30' AND (:transaction_type = 'A' OR :transaction_type = 'U') THEN

Declare ActCode varchar(100);

Declare TestUDF varchar(100);

 

select JDT1."Account"  , PCH1."U_TestUDF" Into ActCode, TestUDF from JDT1 Inner Join PCH1 On JDT1."CreatedBy" = PCH1."DocEntry" where JDT1."TransType" = 18 and PCH1."DocEntry" = :list_of_cols_Val_tab_del;

If (ActCode = '123' or ActCode = '234') And IfNull(TestUdf,'') = '' Then

SELECT '-1' INTO error FROM DUMMY;
SELECT 'Please enter a value in UDF';
END IF;

End If;

Regards

entry.title: Re: Sap AppBuilder IoS compilation error
entry.content: I think that i've found the issue, this seems related to firebase and notifications: i've added the get-token function, uploaded the firebase configuration file in building AND checked the "Push notifications" (without configuring them) in enabled capabilities of the respective provisioning profile in Apple developer portal.

entry.title: Re: Service Consumption Model 2 for OData Client Proxy
entry.content:
Hi @nishantbansal91 

you can call the OData service in ECC from the ABAP Environment on BTP. There are multiple ways to establish the connection. The recommended way is described under "Connecting to a Remote Service" in this blog. However, cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_cloud_destination is also possible. The different options are explained in the SAP help documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/enable-http-communication-in-your-abap-code?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 

Best regards 
Bernhard

entry.title: Text forwarding with different language keys (SD order -> BANF ->PO->EDI-Orders)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have the following problem:

SD order: Customer has defined EN as language key -> Texts are also defined with EN within the order
BANF: Texts are transferred with language key EN
Purchase order: Texts are transferred but with the language key of the supplier (DE)
EDI orders: Texts are not transferred in IDOC because different language keys are used in order + BANF and PO

According to SAP notes it is standard behavior - > are there already customers with similar problems and solutions?

Thanks

Nicolai

entry.title: Re: Advance Script Slow Performance in SAC for Planning D...
entry.content:
How much seconds are being spend on preparation vs execution steps -> you can see in data action monitor?

Can you try adding the account you read from with RESULTLOOKUP() also in the MEMBERSET?
That way the engine doesn't need to fetch them in middle of operation.

Potentially like this:

 

MEMBERSET [d/MID_ACCOUNT_LT_PT] = BASEMEMBER([d/MID_ACCOUNT_LT_PT].[h/parentId] , "FI_CALCOPS_0015"),BASEMEMBER([d/MID_ACCOUNT_LT_PT].[h/parentId] , "FI_CALCOPS_0010")
